authority: local TaxKit documentation and decision-record writes, Git commit and push to main, and read-only remote-ref readback; no provider, registry, publication, release, or deployment operation

checks.check: decision schema and immutable source provenance
checks.result: Five records passed decision-record.schema.json, exact affected-task/task-blocking equality, receipt linkage, and reproduction of every source digest from base commit 7f0e357.
The receipt records 61 tracked public MDX files and navigation with draft metadata without claiming publication, availability, correctness, or accepted-current truth.
An independent evaluator accepted the choice/deferral semantics and downstream blockers after exposing and verifying correction of legacy metadata on the materially revised evidence index.

With all candidate paths staged, repository accounting passed for 555 text and eight binary files from 563 tracked files; Effect language-service, lint, format, 12 skill tests, both Knip graphs, and 23 workspace tasks passed.
After push and fetch, local HEAD and origin/main both resolved to af576849560b0282be827cf130fb835d0176d7ff.

postcondition: TaxKit HGI-207 has accepted, addressable decision records. HGI-202 is blocked on public draft semantics, and HGI-203 through HGI-205 are blocked where candidate and proof-retention contracts require named maintainer choices.
rollback: Revert the validation-receipt commit, then semantic commit af576849560b0282be827cf130fb835d0176d7ff; preserve HGI-200, HGI-201, historical evidence, and public content.
limitations: The decision records do not establish public-site availability, registry publication, deployment, SSR, hydration, production, or external-consumer behavior.
Deferred policy choices remain unresolved and must not be encoded implicitly by downstream controls.
nonClaims: No public content, release artifact, retained proof, provider, registry, publication, deployment, or external system was mutated or proved.
Git publication proves only the committed repository artifact and remote ref identity.
